title: Utahia sileri
taxonomy: Utahia sileri is a genus and species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to the southwestern region of the United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant is commonly found in dry areas with well-drained soil, and it is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive white flowers. Utahia sileri has been classified into the family Apiaceae, which is also known as the carrot or parsley family. The plant is also known to be a part of the order Apiales.
